programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Andrew is singled out by His Royal Highness at a farmers' market, Emma and Rob's first farm wedding and calving time collide, and Callum and Zara host historic sheepdog trials.

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: Billy's new start is on a knife edge, and Alan is tempted back to his old ways. Contains very strong language.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: The Apprentice has a winner! Angela hears from Lord Sugar and his new business partner.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Nick Watt. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The story of Paul McCartney's original Höfner bass and the fan-powered quest to find it. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from south west England compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: During a group hike up Scafell Pike, Mary prepares to speak her heart, but a fateful miscommunication and a sudden storm threaten to throw everything off course.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Jackson rises from child prodigy to superstar, but strange behaviour clouds his success.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In High Wycombe, mother and daughter Liz and Alice battle to raise money for Great Ormond Street Hospital, gambling on vintage fashion, ceramics and a bold sewing machine buy.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion has to navigate his way through an eight bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui takes in the views in Dover. And Tommy is in Lincolnshire to look around a bungalow.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan heads to Ireland's southernmost and largest county to meet up with family, compete in the ancient sport of road bowling and explore a Tibetan Buddhist temple on the coast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the impossible answers.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches. Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quickfire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be alright. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive takes on a potential Olympic champion in the boxing ring, gets under the bonnet of a classic Chevy and bangs the drum for Cuban music.

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities: Istanbul
programme.desc: Using 3D scanning, Michael Scott reveals the invisible secrets of Istanbul's ancient palaces, aqueducts and Roman chariot-racing track.

programme.title: Vintage Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: With Michael Aspel. Discoveries of note include a pair of outsize boots, an important collection of memorabilia from the Nuremburg trials, and an Andy Pandy memento.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From the splendid interior of Gloucester Cathedral, presenter Paul Martin, along with experts Catherine Southon and Philip Serrell, views some exceptional items.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael ends the first leg of his Japan adventure on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Carol and Diarmuid on the hunt for the greatest gardens.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum Clunie kicks off his season at Beechgrove, demonstrating what can be grown from a container in just one season, while Brian looks at how to keep lawns looking great.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In a special programme, Monty gets an exclusive preview of a brand new garden created in Regent's Park, London, to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II.

programme.title: The Travelling Conmen - Spotlight
programme.desc: Exposing scammers who break the hearts of homeowners and convert their misery into cash. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Phil Wang introduces musical comedian Rachel Parris and the charismatic Brennan Reece. Contains some strong language and adult humour.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: The Wicker Man
programme.desc: A puritan police sergeant arrives on a Scottish island in search of a missing girl, who the Pagan locals claim never existed. Contains some sexual content and disturbing scenes.

programme.title: Trump's Immigration Crackdown - Panorama
programme.desc: President Trump's crackdown on immigration has led to a big drop in the number of migrants illegally crossing the border. Are his tough policies working.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent south west England. Comedian and actor Ben Whitehead helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary faces renewed pressure from her mother to find a match.

programme.title: Global Eye: How Pakistan Positioned Itself as a Peace Broker
programme.desc: Farhat Javed looks at Pakistan's role in the US-Iran ceasefire. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Pancake's golden algae go missing, leaving all the Piri friends under suspicion.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: On an exciting trip around planet Proxima, Cosima and Big Lizard record some cosmic nature sounds for Dad.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Weasy family has to tidy up the attic. Luckily, Spicy finds a new sorting robot.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes and the Council of Happiness swap jobs, and when the council fail in their quest, the whole forest is at risk of being flooded by Stinkystuff.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings: World Tour
programme.desc: Grizzy exchanges his indifferent fish with the Lemmings' affectionate and lively fish.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck becomes a very serious librarian and makes it his mission to maintain the peace and quiet - that is until Frog arrives and has other ideas.

programme.title: Game on Grandparents
programme.desc: XP Edition. Top Minecraft and Mario Kart tips from Jelly, Yammy and Slogo to help turn grandparents into gaming legends.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Jacob Lewis and Callum team up to challenge Joe, Adam and the Lewis Sisters to an epic driving test and fashion competition.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Phantom, Fire, Hammer, Nitro v the Gym! Will rubber chickens, silly bird calls, fake gladios or a duel defeat lead to a tap-out.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: 18/04/2026. Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes searching for Patagonia's pumas.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In a special programme, Monty gets an exclusive preview of a brand new garden created in Regent's Park, London, to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Zhao Xintong begins the defence of his crown as the season's showpiece event gets underway. Hazel Irvine introduces live coverage from Sheffield's Crucible Theatre.

programme.title: Women's Six Nations: Scotland v England
programme.desc: Coverage from the second round of the 2026 Women's Six Nations as Scotland play England.

programme.title: Women's Six Nations: Wales v France
programme.desc: Coverage from the second round of the 2026 Women's Six Nations as Wales face France.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, a beautiful 17th-century Palladian house owned by the Marquess of Hertford. Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill.

programme.title: Elizabeth: The Unseen Queen
programme.desc: The story of Queen Elizabeth II in her own words, featuring never-before-seen home movies.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: Steeped in myth and untamed beauty, Dartmoor National Park's vast moorlands provide a a sanctuary for rare plant and animal species as well as a treasure trove for archaeologists.
